title: External Signal - OpenClaw Install Friction
source_reference: Manual fixture based on BEAI install/onboarding discussion and user-facing platform signal category
query_examples: 외부 플랫폼 신호는 어떻게 다뤄야 하지?
사용자가 계속 받는 외부 정보는 Knowledge Loop에 어떻게 넣지?
고객이나 플랫폼에서 들어오는 정보는 언제 알림이나 실행 후보가 되지?
외부 커넥터를 아직 만들면 안 되는 이유는?
summary: Users may continuously receive external signals from platforms, channels, customers, market, development tools, and work systems. For BEAI Package, setup friction and platform-change signals should be classified before any automation or connector is proposed.
observed_facts: External platform or channel information is a separate input family from internal AI work records.
External signals can become watch, brief, alert, review, or action-with-approval candidates.
user_decisions: External information from the user's real work should be included in BEAI Knowledge Loop when it affects their reality.
assistant_judgments: External signal intake should stay manual until source paths, output shape, and approval boundaries are stable.

external_signals.text: Repeated user friction around OpenClaw installation can affect BEAI Package adoption and onboarding design.

knowledge_candidates.text: External signals should be routed as ignore, watch, brief, alert, review, or act_with_approval before becoming execution assets.

execution_assets.text: Before adding any external connector, create a manual external_signal_record fixture and verify routing, safety flags, and next safe action.

traps: Treating external signals as durable memory by default.
Creating cron or connector automation before manual signal quality is proven.
Turning alerts into external actions without approval.
next_actions: Use this fixture to test external signal classification and retrieval indexing.
next_safe_action: Review this external signal route before any connector, cron, agent, memory, or external action.
